note: AI-supply-chain value chains per TW sub-industry. TW codes validated against the live TWSE/TPEx universe. A navigational map — verify before acting.

Foundry.summary: Foundry (晶圓代工) converts IC design files into physical silicon using lithography, etch, deposition, and CMP across 500–1,000 process steps — it is the irreplaceable conversion layer between design IP and working silicon. At leading edge (≤5nm), TSMC holds a near-monopoly on the nodes where AI accelerators, custom ASICs, and inference processors are manufactured.
Foundry.ai_relevance: Every AI accelerator — NVDA H/B/R-series, AMD MI-series, Google TPU, custom hyperscaler ASICs — is fabbed exclusively on TSMC N3/N2; wafer demand from AI grew ~40% YoY in 2025 and is the primary driver of TSMC capacity expansion through 2028. Second-tier foundries (UMC, PSMC, VIS) benefit from trailing-node spillover as AI system BOM requires mature-node analog, power, and I/O chips alongside the leading-edge compute die.

stages.name: Wafer Fab Equipment & Lithography
stages.desc: Capital equipment — lithography, etch, CVD/ALD, CMP, metrology — consumed by foundries to build and expand capacity; no fab runs without this layer.

stages.name: Silicon Wafers & Process Materials
stages.desc: Bare silicon wafers and specialty chemicals (photoresists, CMP slurries, etchants, gases) that are consumed per wafer start.

stages.name: Leading-Edge Foundry (TSMC / Samsung)
stages.desc: N3/N2/1.4nm pure-play and IDM foundries that manufacture AI compute die; the volume and yield at this tier determine GPU/ASIC supply.

stages.name: Mature-Node Foundry
stages.desc: 28nm–0.18µm capacity serving analog, power management, display drivers, I/O, and networking silicon that populates the rest of AI server BOM.

stages.name: IC Design (Fabless Customers)
stages.desc: Fabless and fab-lite chip designers who place wafer orders; their tape-out schedules and node mix dictate foundry revenue and utilisation.

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand sink; largest N3/N2 customer

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: Custom ASIC (XPU) for hyperscalers on N3

players.name: 聯發科
players.role: Edge AI SoC; TSMC N3 volume customer

stages.name: Advanced Packaging & System Integration
stages.desc: CoWoS, SoIC, and OSAT packaging that integrates foundry-made compute die with HBM and substrate into deployable AI accelerator modules.

IC Design.summary: Taiwan IC Design houses create the silicon blueprints — SoCs, controllers, power management ICs, MCUs, and mixed-signal chips — that sit between raw wafer fabrication and finished systems. In the inference stack they supply the glue logic, power delivery, and connectivity silicon that every AI accelerator board, server, and edge-inference device depends on.
IC Design.ai_relevance: Wave-1 demand (GPU/HBM) pulled forward wafer starts at TSMC, which in turn raises tape-out volumes for every IC Design house sharing that foundry capacity; wave-2 is the re-rating of the controller, power, and networking chips that surround the AI die — PCIe retimers (祥碩), PMIC (矽力*-KY), BMC/IPMI (信驊), and high-speed interface controllers — whose content per inference server is 5-10× a commodity laptop BOM.

stages.name: EDA & IP Licensing
stages.desc: Electronic design automation software and licensable hard IP (PCIe/USB/DDR PHYs, SRAM compilers) that IC Design houses must license before taping out.

players.name: Synopsys
players.role: EDA market leader; AI-enhanced design tools

players.name: Arm Holdings
players.role: CPU/NPU IP licensor to most TW SoC houses

players.name: 晶心科
players.role: RISC-V CPU IP for embedded/AI-edge SoCs

players.name: 力旺
players.role: Embedded NVM IP (OTP/MTP) for IoT/MCU designs

players.name: M31
players.role: Mixed-signal PHY IP for advanced nodes

stages.name: Leading-Edge Foundry (Manufacturing)
stages.desc: Wafer fabrication on N5/N4/N3/N2 for AI-oriented SoCs and N28/N16/N12 for controllers/PMICs — the physical substrate IC Design houses buy capacity from.

players.name: 台積電
players.role: Dominant foundry for AI-grade IC Design tape-outs

stages.name: IC Design — Controllers & Connectivity (Core)
stages.desc: Taiwan fabless houses designing the PCIe retimers, USB/Thunderbolt controllers, Ethernet PHYs, and high-speed I/O chips that are the connective tissue of every AI server and edge device.

players.name: 祥碩
players.role: PCIe 5/6 retimer leader; AI server content play

players.name: 瑞昱
players.role: Ethernet PHY/NIC; expanding into 800GbE for AI clusters

players.name: 聯陽
players.role: PCIe/USB bridge ICs for server platforms

players.name: 信驊
players.role: BMC/IPMI controller; sole-source in AI servers

players.name: 安國
players.role: USB/NAND flash controller; edge inference storage

stages.name: IC Design — Power Management & Display (Core)
stages.desc: Taiwan fabless houses supplying PMICs, DC-DC converters, motor drivers, and display driver ICs — the power and signal conditioning layer for AI accelerator boards, edge devices, and panels.

players.name: 矽力*-KY
players.role: High-efficiency PMIC; server/AI board power delivery

players.name: 茂達
players.role: Gate drivers and PWM controllers for power stages

players.name: 致新
players.role: DC-DC converters; power management for servers

players.name: 聯詠
players.role: Display driver IC leader; TCON for edge AI panels

players.name: 天鈺
players.role: Power management ICs for IoT/wearable edge AI

stages.name: AI Accelerator & Platform Silicon (Downstream)
stages.desc: GPU, ASIC, and SoC vendors that are the primary customers consuming Taiwan IC Design output — their design wins directly drive tape-out demand back up the chain.

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand anchor; largest AI silicon volume

players.name: AMD
players.role: MI300X/MI400 AI accelerator; TSMC N5/N3 customer

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: Custom ASIC (XPU) for Google/Meta; PCIe/SerDes buyer

players.name: Marvell Technology
players.role: Custom AI silicon + 800G Ethernet; TSMC N3 customer

players.name: Intel
players.role: Gaudi AI accelerator; Xeon platform anchor customer

stages.name: AI Server & Hyperscaler End-Demand
stages.desc: Server ODMs and hyperscale cloud operators that assemble boards using Taiwan IC Design chips and set the ultimate volume cadence for the entire sub-industry.

players.name: 鴻海
players.role: Largest AI server ODM; NVIDIA GB200 NVL rack integrator

players.name: 廣達
players.role: AI server ODM; NVIDIA and hyperscaler BOM anchor

players.name: 緯穎
players.role: Hyper-scale ODM; cloud-first AI rack designs

players.name: Microsoft (Azure)
players.role: Hyperscaler; Maia ASIC + GB200 rack buyer

players.name: Google (GCP)
players.role: Hyperscaler; TPU v5/v6 + third-party AI cluster buyer

ASIC & IP.summary: Taiwan's ASIC design-services and semiconductor IP cluster handles the full custom-silicon stack: turning a hyperscaler's architectural spec into a tapeout-ready GDS file (design services) or licensing reusable embedded cores (RISC-V, SerDes, NVM IP) that every chip integrates. It sits between the chip architect and the foundry, acting as the silicon translation layer.
ASIC & IP.ai_relevance: The hyperscaler ASIC wave (Google TPU, AWS Trainium, Meta MTIA, Microsoft Maia) is structurally shifting compute spend from merchant GPUs to custom silicon — TrendForce projects custom ASIC shipments +45% in 2026 vs. +16% for GPUs — which flows directly through ASIC design-service houses and IP licensors whose revenue scales with tapeout volume and NVM/SerDes content per die.

stages.name: EDA Tools & Process Design Kits
stages.desc: Software and foundry-supplied PDKs that ASIC designers run every design through; without qualified EDA flows there is no tapeout.

stages.name: Semiconductor IP Licensing
stages.desc: Hard/soft silicon IP cores (RISC-V CPU, SerDes, NVM, SRAM compilers, mixed-signal) licensed to ASIC designers per tapeout or per unit.

players.name: Arm Holdings
players.role: CPU IP monopoly; NPU Ethos in AI SoCs

stages.name: ASIC Design Services (Turnkey / NRE)
stages.desc: Full-turnkey ASIC design houses that take a customer spec from RTL through layout, verification, and tapeout management at TSMC/Samsung nodes — the core bottleneck asset in the hyperscaler custom-silicon boom.

players.name: 世芯-KY
players.role: Hyperscaler ASIC leader; Amazon/Meta key customer

players.name: 智原
players.role: SoC design + IP platform; mid-range ASIC

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: In-house ASIC design for Google/Meta XPUs

stages.name: Leading-Edge Foundry (Tapeout Execution)
stages.desc: TSMC and Samsung execute the physical wafer manufacturing once design-service houses hand off the GDS; the foundry relationship is what makes Taiwan design houses strategically sticky.

players.name: 台積電
players.role: N3/N2 sole-source for AI ASIC production

stages.name: Hyperscaler & AI Chip End Customers
stages.desc: The direct buyers of ASIC design services and IP licenses — hyperscalers designing their own inference/training silicon and fabless AI chip companies.

players.name: Google (Alphabet)
players.role: TPU v6/v7 custom ASIC demand anchor

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU compute demand sink driving ASIC competition

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: XPU ASIC merchant supplier to hyperscalers

Memory.summary: Memory chips — DRAM (HBM, DDR5, LPDDR5) and NAND Flash — provide the working and storage memory that feeds AI accelerators, inference servers, and data-center stacks. Taiwan's role concentrates in NOR Flash (data-retention / code-storage), DRAM modules/SSDs, and the controller ICs that sit between raw die and the host system.
Memory.ai_relevance: Each new AI accelerator generation (HBM3E → HBM4, DDR5, LPCAMM2) demands exponentially more memory bandwidth and capacity per GPU, pulling forward wafer starts and expanding the addressable market for every link in the chain — from specialty NOR/SRAM suppliers to module assemblers to controller IC designers. Wave-1 (SK Hynix / Micron) is fully priced; wave-2 upside sits in the controller IC and module-assembly tiers that benefit from volume growth without needing to win the HBM qualification race.

stages.name: Memory Fab Equipment & Materials
stages.desc: Deposition, etch, CMP, litho, and specialty chemicals/gases required to manufacture DRAM and NAND wafers, including TSV drill for HBM stacking.

players.name: Lam Research
players.role: Etch & dep, critical for HBM TSV

stages.name: Memory Die Producers (Global Leaders)
stages.desc: Vertically-integrated IDMs that design and fab DRAM (including HBM), NAND, and LPDDR at leading nodes; the direct output feeds every downstream memory product.

stages.name: NOR Flash & Specialty SRAM (Taiwan Core)
stages.desc: Taiwan-based memory IDMs and fab-lite players producing NOR Flash (code storage, AI edge/IoT), pseudo-SRAM, and specialty DRAM used in networking, automotive, and inference-edge applications.

players.name: 旺宏
players.role: NOR Flash largest TW IDM, AI-edge exposure

players.name: 愛普*
players.role: Embedded NOR Flash IP, foundry model

stages.name: Memory Controller IC Designers
stages.desc: Fabless IC designers producing NAND Flash controllers, DRAM controllers, and interface ICs that convert raw memory die into addressable SSDs, modules, and managed storage.

stages.name: DRAM Module & SSD Assembly
stages.desc: Assemblers that take commodity DRAM/NAND die plus controllers and package them into DDR5 DIMMs, LPCAMM2, enterprise SSDs, and consumer flash products sold to OEMs and system builders.

players.name: 十銓
players.role: DRAM modules & USB flash, retail brand

stages.name: AI Accelerator & Server Demand Sinks
stages.desc: GPU, ASIC, and server OEM customers that consume memory output — the primary demand driver for every memory type from HBM on AI accelerators to DDR5/LPDDR5 in inference servers.

players.name: 廣達
players.role: Server ODM, DDR5 DIMM volume buyer

players.name: 鴻海
players.role: Server & AI rack ODM, memory pull-through

OSAT.summary: OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) covers the back-end packaging and electrical testing of bare dies into finished chips, sitting between foundry wafer output and system-level integration. For AI inference, this layer is a critical volume bottleneck: every AI accelerator, AI-edge chip, and memory die must pass through assembly and test before integration into servers or advanced packages.
OSAT.ai_relevance: AI accelerator demand — NVDA Blackwell/Rubin, Broadcom/Marvell custom ASICs, edge inference SoCs — drives OSAT volume directly as die counts and I/O complexity grow faster than leading-edge foundry capacity; the wave-2 thesis is that CoWoS overflow forces GPU/ASIC chipmakers to qualify OSAT partners for fan-out and heterogeneous assembly, pulling mid-tier houses (ASE, Amkor, 力成, 京元電) into the AI packaging ramp.

stages.name: 封裝材料 & 設備 (Materials & Equipment)
stages.desc: Supplies the lead-frames, bonding wire, encapsulant mold compound, and process tools consumed in OSAT assembly lines.

stages.name: IC載板 & 基板 (IC Substrate)
stages.desc: Provides the organic substrates and BGA/flip-chip carriers that OSATs use to build up packaged devices.

players.name: 欣興
players.role: IC substrate leader, ABF capacity ramp

stages.name: OSAT核心 — 封裝 (Assembly Leaders)
stages.desc: Full turnkey assembly OSATs performing die attach, wire bond, flip-chip, fan-out, and advanced heterogeneous packaging for AI chips.

players.name: Amkor Technology
players.role: OSAT #2 global, US fab for DoD

stages.name: OSAT核心 — 測試 (Test Services)
stages.desc: Provides wafer probe and final test of packaged semiconductors, a critical yield gate before devices ship to system integrators.

stages.name: AI晶片客戶 (AI Chip Demand Sinks)
stages.desc: GPU, ASIC, and high-bandwidth memory chipmakers that outsource packaging and test to OSAT houses.

Silicon Wafer.summary: Silicon wafer producers grow, slice, and polish monocrystalline silicon ingots into the bare substrates every logic and memory chip is built on. The segment sits one full step upstream of foundry and memory, making it a pure volume play on wafer-start demand.
Silicon Wafer.ai_relevance: AI accelerator ramp forces TSMC and memory fabs to sustain or raise wafer-start volumes at leading-edge nodes (N3/N2 for logic, 1α/1β for HBM DRAM), creating structural pull-through for prime 300mm wafer demand even as trailing-node utilization stays soft. This is the classic wave-2 dynamic: wafer suppliers lagged the GPU/HBM run-up but are now re-rating as N2 capacity additions lock in multi-year supply agreements.

stages.name: Polysilicon & Crystal-Growth Inputs
stages.desc: High-purity polysilicon (11N grade) and Czochralski crystal-pulling equipment that feed ingot growth at wafer makers.

players.name: Ferrotec
players.role: quartz crucibles and CZ furnace components

stages.name: Semiconductor Equipment (Wafer-Shaping)
stages.desc: Wire-saw slicing, lapping, CMP polishing, and epitaxial-reactor equipment used to convert ingots into prime polished wafers.

players.name: Tokyo Electron (TEL)
players.role: epi + CMP equipment for wafer prep

stages.name: Silicon Wafer Producers — Leaders
stages.desc: Global-scale 300mm wafer growers commanding long-term supply agreements with leading-edge foundries and memory fabs.

players.name: SK Siltron
players.role: 300mm wafer global #5, captive to SK Hynix

stages.name: Silicon Wafer Producers — Second Tier / Specialty
stages.desc: Smaller-scale or specialty wafer makers serving mature nodes, epitaxial wafers, and niche compound-semiconductor adjacencies.

players.name: 中美晶
players.role: parent of 環球晶; solar + semi wafers

stages.name: Foundry & Memory — Direct Customers
stages.desc: Leading-edge foundries and memory fabs that consume prime 300mm wafers as their core raw input for chip manufacturing.

players.name: Micron Technology
players.role: HBM + NAND wafer demand, US fab expansion

stages.name: AI Accelerator & Hyperscaler Demand Sink
stages.desc: GPU, ASIC, and HBM end-demand that ultimately prices and volumes the wafer supply chain through fab utilization.

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand sink, Blackwell wafer intensity

players.name: AMD
players.role: MI300/MI400 AI GPU, TSMC N3 wafer pull

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: hyperscaler ASIC, drives TSMC N3 wafer demand

players.name: Google
players.role: TPU v6/v7 custom ASIC, large wafer-start block

players.name: Microsoft / AWS / Meta
players.role: hyperscaler ASIC capex, multi-year wafer pull

Semi Equipment.summary: Semi Equipment encompasses the tools, systems, and fab-infrastructure that physically build semiconductors — from lithography and deposition to etch, inspection, and cleanroom construction. It sits one layer upstream of foundries and memory makers, converting capex budgets into installed capacity.
Semi Equipment.ai_relevance: Every new wafer fab, CoWoS packaging line, and HBM TSV stack requires fresh equipment spend; AI-driven capacity expansions at TSMC, SK Hynix, and Samsung are the primary driver of WFE growth in 2025–2027, making this a wave-2 beneficiary that lags the GPU/HBM demand signal by 12–24 months.

stages.name: Equipment Sub-Components & Materials
stages.desc: Precision-machined parts, vacuum components, abrasives, and specialty gases that feed into equipment OEM assembly lines.

players.name: 中砂
players.role: SiC grinding wheels and CMP rings

stages.name: Fab Facility & Infrastructure
stages.desc: Turnkey cleanroom construction, mechanical/piping/electrical systems, and chemical-gas delivery infrastructure installed before process tools arrive.

players.name: 漢唐
players.role: fab turnkey EPC, mechanical and piping

stages.name: Process Equipment OEMs (Wet / Thermal / CVD)
stages.desc: Front-end wafer process tools — wet clean, etch, diffusion furnaces, CVD chambers — that perform the core chip-making unit processes.

players.name: Lam Research
players.role: etch and HBM TSV tooling leader

players.name: 弘塑
players.role: single-wafer wet clean and etch tools

players.name: 辛耘
players.role: wet clean and photoresist strip tools

players.name: 京鼎
players.role: CVD chamber clean, remote plasma source

stages.name: Wafer Handling, Automation & Ancillary Tools
stages.desc: Reticle pods, wafer carriers, AMHS automation, and specialty process tools that complete the fab workflow around the primary process tools.

players.name: 家登
players.role: EUV reticle pods, FOUP/SMIF sole TW supplier

stages.name: Inspection, Metrology & Test
stages.desc: Optical and electrical inspection tools — AOI, metrology, IC test — that verify process quality at each fab step and at final test.

players.name: 致茂
players.role: test and measurement, power test systems

players.name: 德律
players.role: AOI and ICT board inspection systems

players.name: 牧德
players.role: AOI for PCB and semiconductor, 3D SPI

stages.name: Foundry, Memory & Packaging (Equipment Customers)
stages.desc: The fabs and OSATs that absorb equipment capex to build AI accelerator silicon, HBM memory stacks, and advanced packaging — the direct revenue sink for equipment makers.

players.name: SK Hynix
players.role: HBM memory, High-NA EUV early adopter

players.name: Samsung
players.role: memory and foundry, dual equipment buyer

players.name: Micron
players.role: DRAM and HBM, US fab expansion

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand sink driving all capex

Semi Materials.summary: Semi Materials producers supply the chemical consumables — photoresists, CMP slurries, sputtering targets, ultra-pure etchants, and dielectric/encapsulant resins — that every wafer fabrication and advanced packaging step burns through. As a process-input layer it sits between bulk chemical feedstocks and the fabs/OSAT houses that consume these materials run-rate at every node shrink and every package iteration.
Semi Materials.ai_relevance: AI accelerator ramps at TSMC (N3/N2 CoWoS) and HBM fabs at SK Hynix/Micron drive outsized pull on advanced photoresists, high-k precursors, and CMP slurries because leading-node and 3D-stacking processes consume 2-4x more chemical steps per wafer than mature nodes; wave-1 spend on GPUs and HBM directly translates to a wave-2 run-rate uplift in fab chemical consumption.

stages.name: Chemical Feedstock & Specialty Gas Suppliers
stages.desc: Produce bulk organic/inorganic precursors, ultra-high-purity gases, and fluorine compounds that are the raw inputs to semiconductor chemical formulations.

stages.name: Semi Materials Producers — Process Chemicals
stages.desc: Formulate and supply photoresists, CMP slurries, wet etchants, ultra-pure solvents, and developer chemicals directly consumed inside wafer fabs.

stages.name: Semi Materials Producers — Dielectrics & Encapsulants
stages.desc: Supply epoxy molding compounds, underfill resins, and specialty film laminates used in chip packaging and IC substrate fabrication.

players.name: 長興
players.role: epoxy resins, EMC underfill for advanced packaging

players.name: 律勝
players.role: ABF-type film & CCL prepregs for substrates

stages.name: Wafer Fabs & Memory Fabs
stages.desc: Logic and memory fabs that consume semiconductor chemicals, targets, and slurries at run-rate in advanced node and 3D-stacking processes.

stages.name: Advanced Packaging & OSAT
stages.desc: OSAT and substrate houses that consume encapsulants, underfills, and specialty films to assemble CoWoS, HBM stacks, and 2.5D/3D packages for AI chips.

players.name: 欣興
players.role: IC substrate; consumes ABF film & resins

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand anchor; H100/B200 packaging pull

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: custom ASIC CoWoS ramp; packaging pull

IC Substrate.summary: IC Substrate (IC載板) bridges the bare semiconductor die to the PCB motherboard, providing the fine-pitch interconnect that routes power and signals between chip and package. For AI accelerators it is the foundational layer beneath every CoWoS/SoIC advanced package, making substrate density and warpage control direct constraints on GPU/ASIC package yield.
IC Substrate.ai_relevance: Each successive GPU generation (H100→B200→Rubin) requires larger, denser substrates with finer L/S (below 10/10 µm), directly expanding substrate area per unit and stressing ABF-layer supply; wave-1 CoWoS capacity additions at TSMC pull wave-2 substrate demand from the three TW leaders, creating a lagging bottleneck that hasn't yet been fully priced into the substrate names.

stages.name: ABF & Raw Materials
stages.desc: Ajinomoto Build-up Film (ABF) resin and other key substrate dielectrics, copper foil, and specialty resins that set substrate layer-count and fine-pitch capability.

stages.name: Substrate Equipment
stages.desc: Laser drills, direct-imaging (DI) lithography, plating, and inspection tools required to pattern fine-pitch build-up layers on IC substrates.

stages.name: IC Substrate Producers (Leaders)
stages.desc: Manufacturers of high-end flip-chip BGA and embedded-die substrates used directly under GPU, CPU, and custom-ASIC packages.

players.name: 欣興
players.role: TW IC substrate leader; ABF expansion for AI

players.name: 南電
players.role: Premium ABF substrate; CoWoS supply partner

players.name: 景碩
players.role: IC substrate second-tier; ramp to finer L/S

players.name: Shinko Electric
players.role: IC substrate #2 in Japan; JIC take-private

stages.name: Advanced Packaging (OSAT / Foundry)
stages.desc: Customers that bond bare die onto IC substrates using flip-chip or CoWoS processes, creating the final compute package delivered to board assemblers.

players.name: Amkor
players.role: Advanced packaging; substrate pull for AI

players.name: 力成
players.role: OSAT substrate consumption for HPC modules

stages.name: AI Accelerator & Server Demand
stages.desc: End customers consuming packaged compute modules: GPU/ASIC designers, hyperscalers, and ODM server assemblers whose CapEx programs set the multi-year substrate demand signal.

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand sink; largest ABF substrate pull

players.name: Broadcom
players.role: XPU ASIC substrate consumer for hyperscalers

players.name: 廣達
players.role: AI server ODM; substrate embedded in BOM

players.name: 緯穎
players.role: GPU server ODM; NVDA rack-scale programs

PCB.summary: PCB (印刷電路板) manufacturers etch, laminate, and drill the copper-clad boards that mechanically and electrically interconnect every active component in a server, networking, or storage system. They sit between material suppliers (CCL, copper foil, chemicals) and final system assemblers.
PCB.ai_relevance: AI server build-outs (GB200 NVL72 racks, Ethernet AI-fabric switches, storage backplanes) demand high-layer-count, tighter-trace ABF-adjacent PCBs and ultra-dense flex boards at volumes that are structurally above prior server cycles. Wave-1 captured memory/GPU; wave-2 rotation watches whether PCB ASP lift from AI-spec boards is enough to re-rate the still-cheap mid-tier players.

stages.name: CCL & Raw Laminates
stages.desc: Copper-clad laminate and specialty resin systems are the primary substrate from which PCB layers are built — material chemistry sets loss tangent, thermal performance, and layer registration tolerance.

stages.name: PCB Process Chemicals & Equipment
stages.desc: Wet-chemistry (etching, plating), laser drills, AOI, and press equipment enable fine-line trace formation, via drilling, and multilayer lamination required for AI-spec boards.

players.name: Via Mechanics (日立後身)
players.role: 雷射鑽孔設備主要供應商

stages.name: PCB製造 — 領先梯隊
stages.desc: High-layer-count rigid and rigid-flex PCB producers serving AI server motherboards, GPU carrier boards, and 400G/800G networking switches — highest ASP and technical barrier.

players.name: 健鼎
players.role: 高速伺服器板龍頭，大客戶NVDA/CSP

players.name: 華通
players.role: 高層數伺服器板；ABF+傳統雙線

stages.name: PCB製造 — 第二梯隊 / 利基板
stages.desc: Mid-tier and niche PCB makers supplying networking backplanes, industrial, automotive, and lower-layer-count server boards — cost-competitive feeders to the system supply chain.

players.name: 瀚宇博
players.role: 高階多層板；IC測試板利基

players.name: 楠梓電
players.role: 多層板+背板；伺服器供應鏈

players.name: 泰鼎-KY
players.role: 大陸廠為主；消費+工業板

players.name: 定穎投控
players.role: 中高階多層板；轉型AI板中

stages.name: Server & Networking System Assembly
stages.desc: ODM system integrators and networking OEMs consume AI-spec PCBs as the backbone of GPU trays, top-of-rack switches, and HBM carrier assemblies — the direct demand pull for high-layer-count boards.

stages.name: AI Accelerator & Hyperscaler Demand
stages.desc: GPU and custom ASIC vendors and the hyperscalers that deploy them represent the terminal demand signal — their capex and silicon roadmaps set the PCB spec upgrade cycle.

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand sink；高層板主要拉動

CCL.summary: CCL (Copper Clad Laminate) is the base dielectric substrate from which PCBs are fabricated — glass-fiber cloth impregnated with resin, laminated with copper foil, forming the rigid backbone of every PCB in a server. It sits one stage upstream of PCB fabrication in the AI hardware stack.
CCL.ai_relevance: AI accelerator servers demand high-layer-count, low-Dk/Df CCL (especially halogen-free and high-speed grades like M6/M7) to handle 112G/224G signaling — specs that mainstream commodity CCL cannot meet. As hyperscaler GPU cluster buildout accelerates, AI-grade CCL capacity is the binding material constraint that PCB makers (臻鼎, 健鼎, 華通) cannot substitute away from, making this a textbook wave-2 feeder play.

players.role: Hyperscaler capex; AI cluster demand anchor

Passive Components.summary: Passive components — principally MLCCs, resistors, inductors, and aluminum/tantalum capacitors — are foundational circuit-level elements that filter power, decouple noise, and regulate signals on every PCB in the AI stack. The segment sits immediately upstream of board assembly, feeding server ODMs and networking hardware that house accelerators.
Passive Components.ai_relevance: Each AI server rack carries 5–10× the passive-component density of a conventional server: high-frequency switching power rails for GPU VRMs, decoupling capacitors for SerDes lanes, and bulk capacitance for transient-load buffering during inference bursts. The hyperscaler capex supercycle — concentrated in CoWoS-capped GPU nodes and 800G/1.6T switches — directly expands MLCC and inductor unit demand from the same Taiwan-dominated supply base, with lead times already re-tightening on automotive-grade Hi-CV and EIA-0201 sizes preferred by AI power topologies.

stages.name: Raw Materials & Chemical Inputs
stages.desc: Ceramic dielectric powders (barium titanate), base-metal electrode (BME) nickel paste, palladium/silver for terminations, and aluminum foil for electrolytic capacitors feed the core MLCC and resistor manufacturing process.

stages.name: Passive Component Manufacturers — Leaders
stages.desc: Producers of MLCCs, chip resistors, inductors, and aluminum/tantalum capacitors that are spec'd into AI server, networking, and power delivery BOM.

stages.name: Passive Component Manufacturers — Second Tier
stages.desc: Niche and mid-tier TW-listed producers covering specialty ceramic caps, film capacitors, aluminum electrolytics, and resistor networks for industrial and server applications.

stages.name: Board Assembly & Power Delivery Module Makers
stages.desc: PCB assemblers and VRM/power-module manufacturers that consume passives at highest density per unit — GPU server boards and 800G switch cards are the primary AI-driven end-points.

players.name: 臻鼎-KY
players.role: PCB leader; AI server board supply

players.name: 台達電
players.role: VRM & power modules; GPU rack PSU

stages.name: AI Server ODMs & Hyperscaler Demand Sinks
stages.desc: Server ODMs integrating GPU trays and 800G switch fabric are the ultimate volume demand pull for passives; hyperscaler direct procurement increasingly spec-controls component grades.

players.name: NVIDIA
players.role: GPU demand anchor; BOM spec setter

Connectors.summary: Connectors are the electrical/mechanical interface components linking PCBs, power supplies, cables, and backplanes inside servers, switches, and AI accelerator boards. Taiwan's connector cluster sits at the intersection of precision metal stamping and high-speed signal integrity, supplying into every AI server box assembled by the major ODMs.
Connectors.ai_relevance: AI accelerator nodes (H100/B200 class) require 2-3x more power and signal connectors per board than standard servers — denser GPU cards, NVLink bridges, and 400/800G switch backplanes all demand high-current power connectors and ultra-high-speed differential-pair signal connectors that the legacy server connector spec cannot handle. Wave-2 demand is structural: each incremental GB200 rack deployed consumes high-pin-count power and signal connectors at dollar-content well above prior generations.

stages.name: Raw Materials & Sub-components
stages.desc: Copper alloy strip (terminals), precious-metal plating stock (Au/Pd), and high-performance engineering resins (nylon, LCP) that form connector contacts and housings.

stages.name: PCB & IC Substrate Interfaces
stages.desc: Printed circuit boards and IC substrates that connectors physically mount onto, defining pitch geometry, impedance requirements, and thermal constraints for connector design.

Cooling.summary: Cooling produces heat-dissipation hardware — vapor chambers, heat pipes, cold plates, liquid cooling loops, and fans — that keeps AI accelerators within thermal envelope. It sits between the server ODM assembly stage and the compute substrate, and is a direct physical constraint on GPU/ASIC sustained throughput.
Cooling.ai_relevance: Each Blackwell/Hopper GPU TDP has scaled to 700 W+ per chip and rack-level power densities now exceed 100 kW, making air cooling physically inadequate and forcing datacenter operators into liquid/immersion cooling — a structural step-change in cooling complexity and content-per-rack that directly expands the Taiwan cooling supply chain's addressable value.

stages.name: Thermal Materials & Fluid Inputs
stages.desc: Supplies the base materials consumed in cooling assemblies: copper/aluminium strip, sintered powder for heat pipes, thermal interface materials (TIM), and dielectric coolant fluids.

players.name: Wieland Group
players.role: high-purity copper strip for heat pipes

Server & ODM.summary: Taiwan ODMs design and assemble complete AI servers — GPU baseplates, NVLink/CXL backplanes, thermal sleds, power distribution — then deliver white-box racks to hyperscalers and cloud builders. The segment sits directly between component suppliers (PCB, power, cooling, networking) and the global compute build-out.
Server & ODM.ai_relevance: Each next-gen GPU SKU (H100→H200→B200→GB300) requires a new server chassis design with a tighter power/thermal/signal-integrity spec, forcing ODMs to co-develop with NVIDIA and hyperscalers 12-18 months ahead — giving the few qualified players structural pricing power and repeat design wins that compound as inference cluster density grows.

Networking.summary: Taiwan networking ODMs design and manufacture white-box switches, routers, wireless access points, and CPE — the physical boxes that integrate merchant silicon and optics into deployable network systems. They sit between the chip and the end buyer, capturing margin on system integration and manufacturing scale.
Networking.ai_relevance: Every 100k-GPU AI cluster requires tens of thousands of high-radix Ethernet switches; hyperscalers bypass branded vendors (Arista, Cisco) and source white-box systems directly from ODMs, making Taiwan white-box switch makers like 智邦 (Accton) the volume beneficiary of AI cluster build-outs. The wave-2 logic is that switch port count scales with GPU count, so the ODM tier captures AI capex with a lag behind GPU silicon.

stages.name: Networking Silicon (Switch ASIC / PHY / DSP)
stages.desc: Merchant switch ASICs, PHY chips, and PAM4 DSPs that are the intelligence core of every white-box switch.

Optical Comms.summary: Taiwan's optical comms tier produces laser chips (EML/VCSEL/InGaAs), passive WDM components, fiber assemblies, and sub-rate transceiver modules that feed the global 800G/1.6T pluggable transceiver supply chain. It sits one layer below the branded transceiver module houses and two layers below the AI cluster fabric.
Optical Comms.ai_relevance: Every Blackwell/GB200 rack consumes hundreds of high-speed transceivers, and 1.6T modules are mandatory at >50k-GPU scale; EML laser chips and InGaAs receivers inside those modules are sourced from exactly this tier, where supply is sold out through 2027 — making it the cleanest wave-2 bottleneck after memory.

stages.name: Substrate & Epitaxial Wafer
stages.desc: InP and GaAs compound semiconductor substrates plus epitaxial wafer growth — the raw material for all laser and photodetector chips in optical transceivers.

stages.name: DSP & Driver ICs
stages.desc: High-speed optical DSPs, trans-impedance amplifiers (TIA), and laser drivers that digitally condition signals inside every pluggable transceiver module.

players.name: MACOM Technology
players.role: TIA and driver IC for InP/EML

stages.name: 光通訊零組件 (TW Core Producers)
stages.desc: Taiwan-based makers of EML/VCSEL laser chips, InGaAs photodetectors, TOSA/ROSA sub-assemblies, passive WDM/MUX elements, and fiber-cable assemblies that are built into 400G–1.6T transceiver modules.

players.name: 聯亞
players.role: VCSEL, InGaAs PD, LiDAR; AI optics play

stages.name: Transceiver Module Brands
stages.desc: Branded 800G/1.6T pluggable transceiver module houses that integrate TW-sourced chips and sub-assemblies into finished QSFP-DD/OSFP form factors for hyperscaler and OEM purchase.

stages.name: AI Cluster Infrastructure (Demand Sink)
stages.desc: GPU switch fabrics, AI server platforms, and hyperscaler data-center builds that consume finished transceivers at hundreds of units per rack — the primary demand pull for the entire optical supply chain.

Power Supply.summary: Power Supply makers design and manufacture AC/DC rack PSUs, distributed power modules, and 48V bus converters that regulate and deliver electricity inside AI servers and GPU clusters. They sit between raw power electronics components and the server ODMs that integrate PSUs into finished racks shipped to hyperscaler data centres.
Power Supply.ai_relevance: AI accelerator servers (GB200 NVL72, HGX H100) draw 3–10× the power of a standard 1U server — a single GB200 rack exceeds 120 kW — forcing a full redefinition of rack power architecture from 12V to 48V bus and driving mandatory upgrade cycles at every hyperscaler. This step-change in power density per rack is a direct, sustained unit-ASP tailwind for every Tier-1 PSU maker before a single GPU ship date moves.

stages.name: Power Semiconductors & Magnetic Components
stages.desc: Wide-bandgap FETs (SiC/GaN), power controllers/drivers, and passive magnetics (transformers, chokes) that set PSU efficiency and switching frequency.
